title: Finding Big Moves with Unusual Options Activity
text: What's up outliers? Today we're going to talk about how to use unusual options activity. We're going to talk about what it is, what the profit mechanisms are, how to identify it, and then we're going to talk about specific ways to implement strategies wrapped around this concept. And then I'm going to give you an example. The example we're going to talk about is a trade that made a little over 95 grand in like 3 days. Now, that's really designed to catch attention. There's plenty of versions where these lose, but the broad idea behind unusual options activity, as far as I typically trade it, is they do provide pretty significant riskreward opportunities asymmetrically, meaning we're looking for bigger wins and pretty frequent but smaller losses. So, first off, what is unusual options activity? It's really simple. It's when we see abnormal option trading patterns and things like really high volume just as a relative metric, large blocks, sweeps, which are different kinds of trades that are placed on the institutional side. typically really big changes in open interest IV going off the charts looking at open interest relative to volume which we'll take a look at all of these the main thing to pay attention to in in the example that we're going to look at is if we just eyeball this stock E SB we can see over the last 30 days we can see what the average volume options premium was over different days and

Because a lot of times in the money trades are used for different kind of stock replication or hedging activity that doesn't necessarily associate with directional speculation. So in this case, if I'm looking for directional speculation, I want stuff that's generally out of the money and stuff that's actually relatively closer in time because the only reason why you buy out of the money calls that expire in 30 days is if you think something directionally is going to happen.
